title: Fix GMM bic()/aic() signatures and add to BayesianGaussianMixture
description:
Current FittedGaussianMixture::bic(n_samples) and aic(n_samples) take a sample count. sklearn signature is bic(X) / aic(X) — they consume the data so the per-sample log-likelihood can be re-computed.

Fix: change to take &Array2<F>, internally compute score(X) * n_samples then add the appropriate complexity penalty. Also add the same two methods to FittedBayesianGaussianMixture (current crate does not expose them at all).
